text.title: Pair with AI Agent Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the pair-with-ai-agent-protocol methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Session opens with a written intent + at least one acceptance test the agent must make pass before commit.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit acceptance signal the agent commits whenever it 'feels done'; tests are the only objective stop signal.

rule.statement: Agent halts and asks the human before any rm, drop, migrate, deploy, push, or destructive shell command.
rule.rationale: These commands are unrecoverable; agent confidence does not justify autonomous destruction.

rule.statement: Agent prints the full unified diff and waits for human ratify before invoking git commit.
rule.rationale: The diff is the single artefact a human can review; reviewing the chat log is not the same as reviewing the change.

rule.statement: One session = one task; sessions that grow beyond the original intent are closed and re-opened with the new intent stated.
rule.rationale: Scope creep inside an agent session compounds errors and makes the resulting diff unreviewable.

rule.statement: PR body links the agent transcript (file path or pastebin URL) so reviewers can audit the reasoning trail.
rule.rationale: Reviewers need to spot agent misunderstandings the diff alone hides (assumed schema, hallucinated symbol).

rule.statement: Plan + review use the high tier (opus); routine edit uses mid tier (sonnet); the PR body records which model wrote what.
rule.rationale: Cheap-tier planning produces incorrect plans; expensive-tier mechanical edits waste budget.

rule.statement: If agent usage is inline-only (ghost text) OR the change is doc-only OR repo is greenfield with no users, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Session protocol overhead is wasted when the agent has no power to break anything.
